name: Stop Hacks and Improve Electronic Data Security Act
owner: New York State Office of the Attorney General
description: New York State law (SB 5575/A 5635, enacted 2019, effective 2020-03-21) expanding data breach notification requirements and imposing reasonable security obligations. Codified at NY General Business Law §899-aa (notification) and §899-bb (data security). Applies to any person or business owning or licensing computerized data including private information of NY residents. Defines 'private information' broadly to include biometric data, username/password combinations, account access credentials. Reasonable security must include administrative, technical, and physical safeguards.
relationToCloudSecurity: Cloud providers handling NY residents' data must support customer compliance with SHIELD's reasonable security requirements. Affects breach notification obligations of cloud-using companies.
relationToAISecurity: AI systems processing NY residents' personal information must satisfy SHIELD's reasonable security; biometric AI applications (face/fingerprint) are explicitly covered as 'private information'.

license.notes: US state statutes are public-domain government works and may be freely redistributed.

lifecycle.notes: Effective 2020-03-21 for the security requirement; breach notification provisions effective 2019-10-23.

current_extraction.notes: Metadata-only stub. Source content acquisition + structured extraction is TODO.

scope.applies_to: anyone holding NY residents' private information
